stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, efficient model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the continued relevance and community interest in Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Zero-day exploit completely defeats default Windows 11 BitLocker protections
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A zero-day exploit has been discovered that bypasses default BitLocker protections in Windows 11.
The specific mechanism of the exploit is currently unknown, and Microsoft is investigating.
This represents a significant security vulnerability impacting a core Windows feature.

stories.title: MetaBackdoor: Exploiting Positional Encoding as a Backdoor Attack Surface in LLMs
stories.author: Ahmed Salem
bullet_points: Introduces 'MetaBackdoor,' a novel class of backdoor attack targeting Large Language Models (LLMs).
Challenges the assumption that LLM backdoors must rely on explicit content-based triggers.
Exploits positional encoding within LLMs as a covert and powerful attack surface.

stories.title: Talk is (Not) Cheap: A Taxonomy and Benchmark Coverage Audit for LLM Attacks
stories.author: Alexey A. Shvets
bullet_points: Introduces a novel, reusable framework to audit the collective coverage of LLM attack benchmarks.
The framework uses a 4x6 Target x Technique matrix, grounded in STRIDE, derived from a 507-leaf taxonomy.
The methodology synthesizes insights from 932 arXiv security studies (2023-2026) to validate threat surface coverage.

stories.title: linux 0-day, access root-owned files as an unprivileged user
stories.author: github.com via dzwdz
bullet_points: A zero-day vulnerability has been discovered in the Linux kernel.
This vulnerability allows an unprivileged user to gain access to files owned by the root user.
This represents a critical security flaw requiring immediate attention from system administrators.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The pipeline being showcased is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern deep learning ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for Macs.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed specifically for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in 20B and 120B sizes and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.

stories.title: OpenAI’s Codex is now in the ChatGPT mobile app
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI is integrating Codex, its code-writing AI tool, into the ChatGPT mobile application.
This move is part of OpenAI's effort to compete with rivals like Anthropic's Claude Code.
The update allows users to access Codex's desktop capabilities (like code writing and app usage) on their phones.

stories.title: Your doctor’s AI notetaker may be making things up, Ontario audit finds
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: An Ontario audit revealed significant inaccuracies in AI-powered medical tools.
The AI notetaker system was found to generate fabricated therapy referrals and incorrect prescriptions.
The finding highlights critical safety and reliability concerns regarding the deployment of AI in healthcare.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex AI safety claims mandated by current governance frameworks.
AI governance demands verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are limited to observable model outputs, creating an epistemological gap.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining the core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being 'frozen' after pretraining, allowing for timely, domain-specific knowledge integration.
Encodes new knowledge into a dedicated memory model, offering an efficient alternative to full model fine-tuning.

stories.title: MeMo: Memory as a Model
stories.author: Armando Solar-Lezama
bullet_points: Introduces MeMo (Memory as a Model), a framework to update LLMs without retraining core parameters.
Addresses the limitation of LLMs being static post-pretraining, which hinders real-world, timely application.
Encodes new, domain-specific knowledge into a dedicated memory model for efficient knowledge integration.

stories.title: Forgetting That Sticks: Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in AI safety: the failure of standard unlearning evaluations to account for post-training quantization.
Identifies a systematic dual failure where gradient-based forgetting methods fail under 4-bit compression, while quantization-robust methods achieve minimal forgetting.
Proposes a new framework, 'Quantization-Permanent Unlearning via Circuit Attribution,' to address this interaction between model compression and forgetting.

stories.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform
stories.author: gemini-enterprise
bullet_points: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now generally available.
It is being deployed on the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform.
The model is highlighted for being fast and cost-efficient.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tooling and proprietary API access.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: X agrees to crack down on illegal hate and terror content in the UK
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: X has agreed to new commitments with the UK's online safety regulator, Ofcom.
The commitments focus on better protecting UK users from illegal hate and terror content on the platform.
X plans to withhold UK access to accounts found to be posting illegal terrorist content.

stories.title: Meta brings virtual writing to everyone with Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Meta is expanding functionality on its Meta Ray-Ban Display smart glasses.
A new feature allows users to write messages using only hand gestures.
The gesture-based writing capability will be integrated across major messaging platforms like WhatsApp and Instagram.

stories.title: Claude Code's product lead talks usage limits, transparency, and the "lean harness"
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic's Claude Code product lead emphasizes a 'no grand plan' approach, suggesting a focus on iterative, pragmatic development.
The discussion touches upon practical aspects of LLM deployment, specifically usage limits and transparency.
The concept of a 'lean harness' is mentioned, likely referring to a minimalist or efficient deployment framework for the model.

stories.title: Energy supplier abandons Lake Tahoe residents to serve data centers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Local residents in Lake Tahoe are competing with large data centers for energy supply.
The energy supplier is prioritizing data center needs over the needs of the town's 49,000 residents.
The conflict highlights the growing strain that data center energy demands place on local infrastructure.

stories.title: EntityBench: Towards Entity-Consistent Long-Range Multi-Shot Video Generation
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: Introduces EntityBench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-range, multi-shot video generation.
Addresses the key challenge in video generation: maintaining entity consistency (characters, objects, locations) across long sequences.
The benchmark is derived from real narrative media, offering a more robust evaluation standard than previous methods.

stories.title: ATLAS: Agentic or Latent Visual Reasoning? One Word is Enough for Both
stories.author: Pheng-Ann Heng
bullet_points: The paper discusses two approaches to visual reasoning: direct image generation (computationally expensive) and alternative methods.
It contrasts 'agentic reasoning' (using code/tool calls) with 'latent reasoning' (using hidden embeddings).
A key challenge identified is the context-switching latency associated with agentic methods relying on external execution.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying real-world events chronologically, allowing agents to interact with historical data as it unfolded.
This enables agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while maintaining chronological context.

stories.title: VGGT-Edit: Feed-forward Native 3D Scene Editing with Residual Field Prediction
stories.author: Wentao Zhang
bullet_points: VGGT-Edit introduces a new method for native 3D scene editing using a feed-forward architecture.
It addresses the limitation of current 3D reconstruction models, which struggle with dynamic human instructions.
The approach moves beyond traditional 2D-lifting strategies for scene manipulation.

stories.title: Quantitative Video World Model Evaluation for Geometric-Consistency
stories.author: Xueyan Zou
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of quantitatively evaluating the 3D physical plausibility of generated videos from world models.
Introduces PDI-Bench (Perspective Distortion Index), a new quantitative framework for auditing geometric coherence.
Moves beyond subjective human judgment or learned graders to provide diagnostic metrics for geometric failures in AI video generation.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
Identifies gradient conflict and optimization instability when co-training diverse PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media).
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: OpenDeepThink: Parallel Reasoning via Bradley--Terry Aggregation
stories.author: Jingbo Shang
bullet_points: Introduces OpenDeepThink, a new framework for improving LLM reasoning by scaling compute breadth.
Addresses the 'selection bottleneck' in parallel reasoning by avoiding noisy, pointwise LLM judging.
Uses pairwise Bradley-Terry aggregation to select the best candidate from a population of parallel reasoning traces.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical image models, specifically poor interpretability and lack of historical context.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and structured timing.

stories.title: RefDecoder: Enhancing Visual Generation with Conditional Video Decoding
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in latent diffusion models where decoders are often unconditional despite heavily conditioned denoising networks.
Proposes RefDecoder, an architectural enhancement that applies equal conditioning to the decoder to improve visual generation.
Aims to mitigate loss of detail and inconsistency in video generation by preserving structural integrity.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test adaptive AI agents.
The system achieves this by replaying real-world events chronologically, mimicking real-time information flow.
It allows agents to forecast future events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with a historical timeline.

stories.title: When Are Two Networks the Same? Tensor Similarity for Mechanistic Interpretability
stories.author: Thomas Dooms
bullet_points: Introduces 'tensor similarity,' a new weight-based metric for comparing neural network components.
Addresses limitations of existing similarity measures by being invariant to weight-space symmetries and out-of-distribution behavior.
Crucial for advancing mechanistic interpretability, which seeks to understand the internal computations of large models.

stories.title: Eradicating Negative Transfer in Multi-Physics Foundation Models via Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Routing
stories.author: Arastu Sharma
bullet_points: Addresses 'negative transfer' in scaling Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) foundation models.
The core problem is gradient conflict when co-training disparate PDE regimes (e.g., fluid dynamics vs. porous media flows) in dense models.
Proposes a solution using Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing to manage incompatible spectral and geometric demands.

stories.title: Evidential Reasoning Advances Interpretable Real-World Disease Screening
stories.author: Jing Qin
bullet_points: Introduces EviScreen, a new evidential reasoning framework for disease screening.
Addresses limitations in current medical imaging models, specifically lack of interpretability and poor performance.
Leverages region-level evidence from historical cases to provide transparent reasoning pathways in clinical screening.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to bridge the gap between narrative and tabular data.

stories.title: Position: Behavioural Assurance Cannot Verify the Safety Claims Governance Now Demands
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Behavioral assurance methods are insufficient to verify complex safety claims required by current AI governance frameworks.
Governance demands verifiable evidence for properties like absence of hidden objectives and bounded catastrophic capability.
Current assurance techniques (behavioral evaluations, red-teaming) are epistemically limited to observable model outputs.

stories.title: Hand-in-the-Loop: Improving Dexterous VLA via Seamless Interventional Correction
stories.author: Ruoshi Wen
bullet_points: The paper addresses compounding errors in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models when performing dexterous manipulation.
A key challenge identified is the command mismatch during human takeover in Interactive Imitation Learning (IIL) for high-DoF robotic hands.
The proposed 'Hand-in-the-Loop' method aims to improve VLA performance by enabling seamless interventional correction.

stories.title: Self-Distilled Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Yongliang Shen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of coarse, trajectory-level reward signals in Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLM agents.
It introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation (OPSD) to provide dense, token-level guidance using a teacher branch with privileged context.
A key challenge identified is the instability of transferring OPSD to multi-turn agents due to compounding supervision issues.

stories.title: FutureSim: Replaying World Events to Evaluate Adaptive Agents
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Introduces FutureSim, a new simulation framework designed to test AI agents in dynamic, real-world environments.
The core innovation is replaying chronological sequences of real-world events (e.g., news articles) to ground agent testing.
Allows agents to forecast future world events beyond their training data cutoff while interacting with historical context.

stories.title: Is Grep All You Need? How Agent Harnesses Reshape Agentic Search
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitations in current agentic search systems, specifically the lack of systematic comparison between retrieval strategy and agent architecture.
It focuses on how Large Language Model (LLM) agents autonomously perform complex tasks by retrieving information, calling tools, and reasoning over large datasets.
The research aims to provide practical insights into how different retrieval methods interact with the agent's overall design and tool-calling mechanisms.

stories.title: Text Knows What, Tables Know When: Clinical Timeline Reconstruction via Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment
stories.author: Jeremy C. Weiss
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of reconstructing precise clinical timelines from patient data.
It combines unstructured clinical narratives (rich context) with structured EHR data (temporal precision).
The proposed method uses Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Alignment to fuse these disparate data types for better patient trajectory modeling.

stories.title: MemEye: A Visual-Centric Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Agent Memory
stories.author: Ruixiang Tang
bullet_points: Introduces MemEye, a new evaluation framework for multimodal agent memory.
Addresses a gap in current research where agents often rely only on textual traces instead of preserving fine-grained visual evidence.
Focuses on testing agents' ability to reason over changing visual states, which is currently underrepresented in evaluations.

stories.title: Show HN: Find the best local LLM for your hardware, ranked by benchmarks
stories.author: andyyyy64
bullet_points: The post introduces a tool or resource for users to find the optimal local Large Language Model (LLM).
The ranking is based on performance benchmarks tailored to specific hardware configurations.
This addresses the growing trend of running AI models locally, a key area in decentralized AI.

stories.title: UK government replaces Palantir software with internally-built refugee system
stories.author: cdrnsf
bullet_points: The UK government is reportedly replacing Palantir software with a proprietary, internally developed system.
The system in question is related to refugee management and processing.
This indicates a shift towards increased government reliance on in-house technology solutions over major external vendors like Palantir.

stories.title: Show HN: GlycemicGPT – Open-source AI-powered diabetes management
stories.author: jlengelbrecht
bullet_points: GlycemicGPT is an open-source, self-hosted AI platform designed for diabetes management.
It integrates data from various sources, including Dexcom G7 and insulin pumps, for personalized analysis.
The project addresses a gap in healthcare by allowing users to run AI analysis on their own infrastructure.

stories.title: Mullvad exit IPs as a fingerprinting vector
stories.author: tmctmt.com via bedrovelsen
bullet_points: Mullvad is actively removing its exit IP addresses.
This action is taken because these IPs have been identified as a vector for user fingerprinting.
The story addresses a privacy and anonymity concern within the VPN/security sector.

stories.title: Building "Sweets Vault" - a multimodal Gemini Agent with physical hardware integration
stories.author: rsamborski
bullet_points: The project involves building a multimodal Gemini Agent named "Sweets Vault."
It features integration with physical hardware, moving beyond purely software solutions.
The application targets an educational use case: motivating young children (seven-year-olds) for reading and handwriting practice.

stories.title: How to A/B Test LLM Prompts Without Breaking Production
stories.author: benchwright
bullet_points: Addresses the critical operational risk of prompt changes destabilizing production systems.
Provides a guide on safely A/B testing Large Language Model (LLM) prompts.
Highlights that prompt iteration can be a more significant production risk than model version updates.
